events.title: Lebanon to seek ceasefire extension in US-hosted talks with Israel
events.description: The United States will host a second meeting between Lebanese and Israeli envoys on Thursday, with Beirut seeking the extension of a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah, a day after Israeli strikes killed at least five people including a journalist. The US-mediated ceasefire, which is set to expire ​on Sunday, has yielded ‌a significant reduction in violence, but attacks have continued in southern Lebanon, where Israeli troops have seized a self-declared buffer ⁠zone. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Angela Diffley tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon to seek ceasefire extension in US-hosted talks with Israel

events.title: Mexico reopens famed pyramids under heavy police presence after deadly shooting
events.description: Mexico's ancient ⁠Teotihuacan archaeological ​site reopened on Wednesday under heavy security and with fewer tourists than usual, two days after a ​gunman killed a Canadian woman and wounded 13 others at the Pyramid of the Moon in a rare attack at a major tourist destination. Monday's shooting has raised scrutiny of security measures at ​Mexico's cultural ‌sites just as the country prepares to co-host the World Cup.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Mexico reopens famed pyramids under heavy police presence after deadly shooting

events.title: How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire
events.description: Since Chernobyl, Ukraine has lived in the shadow of nuclear disaster. Now, with Russia targeting its power grid, it's facing a new nuclear risk. The response is reshaping how the world thinks about energy in wartime.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 How Ukraine is rebuilding its energy system under fire

events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn
events.description: Extreme heat is pushing global food and farming systems to the brink, threatening the livelihoods of over a billion people as rising temperatures and more frequent heatwaves redefine how food is produced worldwide, a new UN report warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Extreme heat pushing global food systems to the brink, UN agencies warn

events.title: Who is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran’s new IRGC chief tied to global attacks and ‘Death to America’ ideology
events.description: Shadowy IRGC commander Ahmad Vahidi is emerging as one of Iran's most powerful figures as Trump's ceasefire deadline approaches, analysts warn.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who is Ahmad Vahidi? Iran’s new IRGC chief tied to global attacks and ‘Death to America’ ideology

events.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
events.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike

events.title: Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages
events.description: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks
events.description: The Security Council meets in emergency session at 3pm to address the escalating conflict in Ukraine. The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March and mid-April, more than 5,000 drones and missiles were launched, killing dozens of civilians and injuring hundreds more. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users can follow here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks

events.title: Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity
events.description: ICC judges say there are substantial grounds to believe Duterte guided anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands The former president of the Philippines, Rodrigo Duterte , will face trial at the international criminal court (ICC) after judges unanimously confirmed charges of crimes against humanity over his “war on drugs”. Pre-trial judges concluded on Thursday that there were substantial grounds to believe Duterte was responsible for the crimes against humanity of murder and attempted murder in relation to anti-drugs crackdowns that led to the killing of thousands of people. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines’ ex-president Rodrigo Duterte to face trial for crimes against humanity

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition
events.description: The Security Council is meeting now at a critical juncture for Syria’s fragile transition. While the Iran-US ceasefire has temporarily eased regional spillover from Lebanon and elsewhere, conditions remain volatile in Syria. The reported US military withdrawal now completed, marks a milestone, yet the chamber faces a country grappling with deep internal fissures and external pressures. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users should click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition

events.title: Timor-Leste parliament questions president over proposed resort’s links to ‘scam’ empire
events.description: Jose Ramos-Horta urged by opposition to explain diplomatic passport given to businessman behind resort project, who denies any involvement with organised crime Timor-Leste’s opposition has questioned how foreign investors in a proposed cryptocurrency resort obtained prime beachfront real estate in the country’s capital, and has called on the president to explain why he issued a diplomatic passport to a Chinese businessman involved in the project. Speaking in parliament in Dili on Monday, Fretilin opposition party MP Florentino Ximenes da Costa “Sinarai” raised concerns about the proposed AB Digital Technology Resort, which was the subject of a months-long investigation by the Guardian and Organised Crime and Corruption Reporting Project (OCCRP) . Continue reading...

events.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
events.description: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

events.title: ‘The world’s most impossible job’: Who will lead the United Nations next?
events.description: Candidates to become the next head of the United Nations vowed on Wednesday to revitalise the troubled organisation through reform, while upholding its core principles of peacekeeping and development. Four candidates are vying to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN secretary-general from the start of next year. The winner will face the daunting task of restoring the credibility of an organisation in crisis, whose global influence has significantly declined in recent years. For more, FRANCE 24’s Oliver Farry is joined by Daniel Safran-Hon, former official in the Office of the Secretary-General and expert on UN affairs.

events.title: War in Lebanon: Most people 'are severely traumatised', CARE country director says
events.description: Lebanon's country director for the charity CARE has told FRANCE 24 that life for the Lebanese is a perpetual challenge that people have to overcome. Michael Adams says the ceasefire there is very fragile, and the constant infractions mean people don't have confidence in it. He told us about how many people have to live either in the streets, or in cars, parks or stadiums, and how so many of them are traumatised by the conflict. He spoke to us in Perspective.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War in Lebanon: Most people 'are severely traumatised', CARE country director says

events.title: Governments failed to deliver $160m of river improvements including for now-parched NSW wetlands, report finds
events.description: NSW and Queensland governments ‘severely underdelivered’ on promised infrastructure to improve water flows, independent review finds Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Two state governments have drastically underdelivered more than $160m in infrastructure measures to improve river health in the northern Murray-Darling basin eight years since they were promised, a major independent review has found. This includes failure by the New South Wales government to secure any of the private land access needed to improve water flows over floodplains in the state’s Gwydir region, where scientists had to scramble to rescue turtles in dried up wetlands last week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Governments failed to deliver $160m of river improvements including for now-parched NSW wetlands, report finds

events.title: Long hours, stress and harassment are causing hundreds of thousands of early deaths, says UN labour agency
events.description: More than 840,000 people die each year from health conditions linked to risks such as long working hours, job insecurity, workplace harassment and bullying, according to a new report by the International Labour Organization (ILO).

events.title: Child reunited with mom after transgender dad, partner allegedly kidnap abroad amid gender surgery fears: feds
events.description: Federal authorities allege a Utah child was taken to Cuba during a planned camping trip, sparking an international parental kidnapping investigation.

events.title: Los Angeles becomes first major US school district to limit classroom screen time
events.description: The school district, which serves about half a million students, is looking to reduce its reliance on devices after the pandemic.

events.title: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
events.description: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counsel A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers. Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang

events.title: Mexico pyramid shooter who took hostages and killed 1 is identified
events.description: Officials identified the gunman who fatally shot a Canadian tourist and wounded others atop a historic pyramid at Teotihuacan in Mexico on Monday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mexico pyramid shooter who took hostages and killed 1 is identified

events.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to stand trial at ICC over deadly ‘war on drugs’
events.description: The International Criminal Court on Thursday confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine president Rodrigo Duterte, paving the way for a landmark trial. Duterte is accused of murder and attempted murder linked to his anti-drugs campaign, in a major test for the court amid mounting political pressure.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ex-Philippine president Duterte to stand trial at ICC over deadly ‘war on drugs’

events.title: Giorgia Meloni's government responds to suggestion of Italy replacing Iran at World Cup
events.description: Italy's government has ruled out any suggestion the country could replace Iran at this summer's World Cup due to the war in the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Giorgia Meloni's government responds to suggestion of Italy replacing Iran at World Cup

events.title: UK’s elite soldier unit faces exodus over ‘lawfare’ fears — warning sign for US military?
events.description: Britain's elite SAS faces a growing exodus as soldiers resign over fears of legal scrutiny for actions in Afghanistan and other conflicts, report says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK’s elite soldier unit faces exodus over ‘lawfare’ fears — warning sign for US military?

events.title: Number of billionaires globally could reach 4,000 in next five years
events.description: There are now 3,110 billionaires but analysis shows ‘deep structural acceleration’ in wealth creation around world The number of billionaires in the world could reach nearly 4,000 by 2031, figures suggest, as the super-rich accumulate wealth at an accelerating rate. There are now 3,110 billionaires globally, according to analysis by the estate agent Knight Frank. This is forecast to rise by 25% over the next five years, taking the total to 3,915. Continue reading...

events.title: Europe cancels thousands of flights amid Middle East fuel shock
events.description: Soaring fuel costs as a result of the conflict in the Middle East are sending the aviation industry into a tailspin, with European airlines being forced to cancel thousands of flights and hike ticket prices. From grounded fleets in Germany to rising fares in France, we look at how the energy crisis is threatening to derail summer travel plans across the continent

events.title: Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil killed in Israeli strike on a house where she took cover, paper says
events.description: Rescue workers say a Lebanese journalist was killed in an Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil killed in Israeli strike on a house where she took cover, paper says

events.title: Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: The United States’ decision to extend a fragile ceasefire with Iran has kept a narrow window open for diplomacy, but fresh security incidents in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday underscore the volatility of the situation and the risks to global shipping and regional stability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Nature’s last strongholds: Why UNESCO sites are a lifeline for a planet in peril
events.description: In a world where the drumbeat of environmental loss is growing louder, a new landmark report from UNESCO has revealed a rare glimmer of hope. While global wildlife has plummeted by a staggering 73 per cent since the 1970s, life within the UN science and culture agency’s protected territories is holding its ground.

events.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard sidelines president as military grip expands
events.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps effectively has assumed control over key state functions and blocked presidential appointments, according to an Iran International report.

events.title: Student allegedly jailed in China for six years after taking part in pro-democracy protests in Australia
events.description: Exclusive: The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect overseas students from political repression The Australian government has been urged to take stronger action to protect Chinese international students from political repression by authorities on their return after a Chinese student was allegedly sentenced to six years’ imprisonment for joining pro-democracy protests in Australia. The student, who the Guardian has chosen not to name, lost contact with his friends in Sydney after returning to China in December 2024. Continue reading...

events.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
events.description: Following the return of Russia to the Biennale, the EU said it will carry out its threat to cut its funding from the international art show.

events.title: School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes
events.description: Iran's schools are closed because of the war. Classes are being held via TV programs and the national intranet. Children without a laptop or smartphone are left out. The country is effectively cut off from the internet.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 School's out in Iran: No phone, no internet, no classes

events.title: Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal
events.description: Libya’s political leaders continue to lag in implementing a roadmap that leads to national elections and unified institutions, the UN Special Representative for the country told the Security Council on Wednesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal

events.title: More than 500 people killed in Tanzania election violence, inquiry finds
events.description: Opposition parties accused security forces but the official report does not say who was responsible.

events.title: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
events.description: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said. Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers with lower stockpiles than usual, CEO Goh Miah Kiat told Reuters in an interview on Tuesday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex

events.title: Prince Harry makes surprise visit to Ukraine
events.description: Prince Harry has arrived in Kyiv, ahead of taking part in the Kyiv Security Forum. His trip comes as Ukrainians fear they could be forgotten with the world's attention turned to the US conflict with Iran.

events.title: Photo of migrant family separation in US scoops World Press prize
events.description: A picture of an Ecuadoran family separated when US immigration agents arrested the father following a court hearing won the 2025 World Press Photo of the Year Award on Thursday. The photo, taken by Carol Guzy of the ZUMA Press agency and iWitness for the Miami Herald, shows the moment Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers hauled away Luis, as his family claws at his shirt to keep him back.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Photo of migrant family separation in US scoops World Press prize

events.title: Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents
events.description: Mexico to investigate possible breach of its constitution and assess US’s role in anti-drug operation near Chihuahua Mexico has launched an investigation into a possible breach of its constitution as it was reported that two US embassy officials who died in a car accident while returning from a raid on a drug lab with local officials in the border state of Chihuahua were CIA operatives. The accident happened early on Sunday, as the officials were driving back from the scene of the raid. Their vehicle skidded off the road and plunged down a 200 metre ravine in the mountains near Chihuahua’s border with the state of Sinaloa. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Two US officials who died after Mexico drug raid reported to be CIA agents

events.title: Florida man's execution date set for killing 2, including small child

events.title: Workers killed and many treated after refinery chemical release as investigators probe cause

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Reza Pahlavi calls for regime change in Iran on Berlin visit
events.description: The former Shah's son is in Germany to drum up support for his global campaign to overthrow Iran's leadership. During a speech in the capital, he called the German government's refusal to meet with him "a disgrace."

events.title: Gaza: Human development set back 77 years as recovery costs rise to $71 billion

events.title: Rinehart’s $200m donation to convert homes for veterans welcomed by RSL – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Australians ‘uneasy’ about NDIS cuts amid $53bn in new defence spending, Mark Butler concedes Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast James Valentine’s family has released a statement after his death. Here’s what they had to say: James passed peacefully at home surrounded by his family, who adored him. Throughout his illness, James did it his way, which lasted all the way until the end when he made the choice to do Voluntary Assisted Dying. Both he and his family are grateful he was given the option to go out on his own terms. He was calm, dignified as always and somehow still making us laugh. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Rinehart’s $200m donation to convert homes for veterans welcomed by RSL – as it happened

events.title: Trump officials consider sending 1,100 Afghans who aided US forces to Congo
events.description: Discussions reportedly come after Trump’s decision to stop initiative that allowed group to apply to resettle in the US The Trump administration is in discussions to potentially send up to 1,100 Afghans who helped US forces during the war in Afghanistan to the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), a non-profit confirmed on Tuesday. The resettlement talks, first reported by the New York Times , come after Donald Trump’s decision to stop an initiative that allowed Afghans who assisted US war efforts to apply to resettle in the US. Continue reading...

events.title: Lebanon says Israel targeted journalist killed in airstrike

events.title: Denmark: Two trains collide injuring several
events.description: Two trains have crashed into each other in Denmark, leaving several people critically injured, authorities have said.

events.title: EU approves massive loan to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto
events.description: The European Union on Thursday approved a massive loan package to help Ukraine meet its economic and military needs for the next two years, the bloc’s Cypriot presidency said, after Hungary lifted its veto. The EU also approved a new raft of sanctions against Russia over its war on Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Europe Editor Armen Georgian tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 EU approves massive loan to help Ukraine after Hungary lifts its veto

events.title: UN leadership hopefuls stress need for peace and restoring confidence
events.description: Contenders for next UN secretary-general made their case this week for a United Nations more invested in peace, but avoided taking positions that could antagonize the member states who will choose the world body's next chief. Chile's Michelle Bachelet, Argentina's Rafael Grossi, Costa Rica's Rebeca Grynspan and Senegal's Macky Sall are all hoping to succeed Antonio Guterres on January 1, 2027, when his second five-year term ends. Each candidate spent three hours this week answering wide-ranging questions from the 193 member states and representatives of civil society.

events.title: Mexican president in the dark about drug operation after which US, Mexican officials were killed

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows

events.title: ‘A history of the Earth’: Twelve Apostles revealed to be as old as 14m years
events.description: Tectonic plate movements over millions of years have lifted and tilted the layers, with records of ancient earthquakes in the rocks Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Microscopic fossils embedded in limestone have helped reveal the true age of Victoria’s Twelve Apostles, as 8.6 to 14m years old. The conclave of giant golden pillars is visited by 2.8 million tourists each year, a highlight for those travelling along the Great Ocean Road south-west of Melbourne. Continue reading...

events.title: AI robot beats elite table tennis players in 'pivotal breakthrough'

Le Monde looks at the important role of priests in the war in Ukraine and we also look at a suggestion to rename the Donbas to "Donnyland", in a bid to soften Donald Trump's hardline stance. events.title: Carney names broad team to advise on tense US-Canada trade talks
events.description: Conservatives and former provincial premiers among those PM names to advisory committee on economic relations Canada’s prime minister, Mark Carney , says his new advisory committee on economic relations with the United States will draw on the “best advice and the broadest perspectives” as the country braces for what many expect will be tense trade negotiations with its southern neighbour. The 24-member advisory committee, announced on Tuesday, shows the prime minister’s eagerness to reach across the political spectrum to ensure Canada is “well positioned to advance its interests” at the looming trade talks. Continue reading...

events.title: After collapse and controversy, Adelaide writers’ week has a new director: ‘I don’t envy anyone in this position’
events.description: Rosemarie Milsom, who formed and runs Newcastle writers festival, will take over from Louise Adler after the literary festival imploded over invitation to Randa Abdel-Fattah In January, as the implosion of Adelaide writers’ week made headlines around Australia and the world, Rosemarie Milsom was watching closely. The Adelaide festival board, which oversees AWW, had overridden the literary festival’s director, Louise Adler, and disinvited the Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah over past comments she’d made about Israel and Zionism. This decision resulted not in a quieter, less-controversial festival as the board members may have hoped, but a boycott by 200-odd writers , the resignation of Adler – followed by the whole board – a potential defamation lawsuit against the South Australian premier and the collapse of AWW. Continue reading...

events.title: World Press Photo announces Photo of the Year 2026

He also emphasised that the programme "works equally well in Africa", following the launch of a regional food distribution hub in Ghana's capital Accra.

events.description: One pilot ordered to repay some of the $600,000 of damage caused by collision in 2021 South Korea’s air force has apologised for a 2021 mid-air collision involving two fighter jets, a day after auditors said pilots were taking selfies and filming during the flight and held them responsible for the accident. “We sincerely apologise to the public for the concern caused by the accident that occurred in 2021,” an air force spokesperson said in a press briefing. The spokesperson said one of the pilots involved had been suspended from flying duties, received severe disciplinary action and has since left the military. Continue reading...

events.title: St Vincent and Grenadines government pauses constitutional amendment bills after public backlash
events.description: Bills are seeking to change section that opposition says makes Godwin Friday, a dual citizen, ineligible to be PM The St Vincent and the Grenadines government has delayed a controversial effort to amend a section of the country’s constitution that the opposition says renders the prime minister ineligible for his position in parliament. Two bills, among six listed for the parliament session on Tuesday this week, were aimed at clarifying a section of the 1979 constitution governing the citizenship eligibility of members of parliament. events.title: Libya oscillates between cooperation and illusion

events.title: Climate scrubbed from G7 meeting to appease US, host France says
events.description: Environment ministers from G7 nations launched a two-day meeting in Paris on Thursday with climate change kept off the agenda to avoid a row with the United States. The office of France's ecology minister Monique Barbut had said the Group of Seven would focus on "less contentious issues" to appease its largest and most powerful member, drawing criticism from activists. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan tells us more.

events.title: Zelensky to join EU leaders in Cyprus after 90-bn-euro loan unblocked
events.description: EU leaders will welcome Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday in Cyprus to celebrate the release of a 90-billion-euro ($105 billion) loan for Kyiv after a prolonged deadlock. Preliminary approval for the badly needed money came on Wednesday but a definitive sign-off is expected by Thursday, before European Union leaders hold talks with Zelensky in Cyprus's Ayia Napa marina. The funds were blocked after a bitter row broke out between Hungary's nationalist premier Viktor Orban and Zelensky.

Janet Fordham was cheated of her life savings and her home over a period of five years by fraudsters apparently based in the UK, Germany, the US and Ghana, the inquest in Exeter was told. events.title: Charlize Theron joins chorus of disapproval over Timothée Chalamet’s ballet comments
events.description: The former ballet dancer said Chalamet’s comments were ‘reckless’ in an interview with the New York Times in which she also discussed her violent childhood Actor and former ballet dancer Charlize Theron has joined the chorus of disapproval aimed at Timothée Chalamet over his remarks that appeared to disrespect performers of ballet and opera . In an interview with the New York Times , Theron said: “Oh, boy, I hope I run into him one day,” adding: “That was a very reckless comment on two art forms that we need to lift up constantly because, yes, they do have a hard time. But in 10 years, AI is going to be able to do Timothée’s job, but it will not be able to replace a person on a stage dancing live.” Continue reading...

events.description: President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.

events.title: Germany news: Bavarian police arrest suspected spies
events.description: Two men have been arrested in Bavaria and charged with espionage, Frankfurt Airport's new Terminal 3 has opened its doors, and former US President Obama has praised a German climate activist. DW has more.

events.title: Bayern Munich on the brink of historic 'double treble'
events.description: With Bundesliga titles in the bag and cup finals to come, Bayern's men and women have a chance to achieve something unique in Europe. The two teams dominate in Germany and appear united in their Champions League aims.

events.title: India news: Tamil Nadu, West Bengal cast votes
events.description: Polling has begun in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal in high-stakes assembly elections.

events.title: Palantir: Why its political manifesto is causing a stir
events.description: The US tech company has posted a 22-point manifesto on X that goes well beyond typical corporate communications. Critics say it is evidence of "technofascism."

events.title: UK, France agree 3‑year deal to curb Channel crossings
events.description: For the first time, the deal links some UK payments to France to performance in stopping migrants. Funding will be halted after one year if targets are not met.

events.title: Nigerian artist Eniwaye Oluwaseyi explores memory, identity in Paris show
events.description: With a new double exhibition called “Buried roots up in the air”, held in Paris and Luxembourg, Nigerian artist Eniwaye Oluwaseyi explores his personal memories through multi-layered compositions that question belonging, identity and cultural roots. FRANCE 24’s Aurore Cloé Dupuis met him in the heart of Paris.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nigerian artist Eniwaye Oluwaseyi explores memory, identity in Paris show

events.title: President Macron inaugurates lithium mine as France, EU seek energy sovereignty
events.description: France is going back to the mines. President Emmanuel Macron on Wednesday visited a facility in central France run by Imerys, the leading French producer of industrial minerals and which is spearheading a major lithium mining project for battery production. The project promises to create hundreds of jobs and comes as France seeks to ensure its own energy sovereignty.

events.title: NGOs file ‘human trafficking’ complaint against Deliveroo, Uber Eats
events.description: Associations that support food delivery workers here in France have filed a criminal complaint for "human trafficking" against the platforms Deliveroo and Uber Eats. One of the attorneys accused the companies of making significant profits by exploiting their workers.

events.title: Washington uses Iraq's own oil money to bend Baghdad to its will
events.description: The Trump administration has blocked a shipment of nearly $500 million in cash to Iraq, pressing Baghdad to dismantle powerful Iranian-backed armed groups. Analysts say the blockage risks destabilising an already fragile economy.

events.title: Second French peacekeeper dies in Lebanon after ambush blamed on Hezbollah
events.description: A second French soldier died on Wednesday from wounds suffered in a weekend ambush against UN peacekeepers in Lebanon blamed on Hezbollah, President Emmanuel Macron said. A first soldier, staff sergeant Florian Montorio, was shot dead in the Saturday ambush, for which Hezbollah has denied responsibility.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Second French peacekeeper dies in Lebanon after ambush blamed on Hezbollah

events.title: The robot that can outplay elite table tennis players
events.description: A robot has become so good at playing table tennis that at times it defeated elite human players, in what has been hailed as "a longstanding milestone for AI".

events.title: British Army uses civilian ferry to take more than 1,400 troops to Europe
events.description: The British Army has used a civilian ferry to send troops to mainland Europe en masse for the first time in a generation.

events.title: Iran’s exiled crown prince Reza Pahlavi splattered with red liquid in Berlin
events.description: Iran’s exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was splattered with red liquid as he left a building after a news conference in Berlin

events.title: Pope wraps up an Africa visit for the history books with a Mass in Equatorial Guinea
events.description: Pope Leo XIV is wrapping up his African odyssey with a final Mass in Equatorial Guinea

events.title: Britain and France sign a 3-year deal to curb small-boat channel crossings
events.description: Britain and France have signed a new multimillion-euro deal to reduce migrant crossings in the English Channel

events.title: Zambia says it has custody of ex-president's body in dispute with family over burial
events.description: A dispute over the remains of Zambia's former President Edgar Lungu continues as a court orders his body returned to his family

events.title: Canada's prime minister says the US does not get to dictate terms for a trade agreement
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says that Washington cannot dictate the terms of the continental trade deal known as USMCA

events.title: Time to apply the brakes to runaway AI, says pioneer
events.description: If AI is “a very fast car with no steering wheel” then regulation must provide one, insists Nobel laureate and Artificial Intelligence pioneer Geoffrey Hinton, the visionary scientist widely known as the “godfather” of the self-learning tech.

events.title: Economic collapse pushes highly educated Gazans into the ‘survival economy’
events.description: Young Palestinians in Gaza with university-level educations are setting aside dreams of putting their hard-won skills into practice and doing whatever they can to survive.

events.title: Secretary-General hopefuls make their case in televised ‘interactive dialogues’
events.description: The four candidates so far in the running to be the next UN Secretary-General will each have the chance to show why they are the best choice during a series of “interactive dialogues” taking place at UN Headquarters in New York this week.

events.title: 'Pro-life Spiderman' sentenced to prison for climbing Las Vegas Sphere
events.description: A Nevada judge sentenced Pro-life Spider-Man climber Maison Des Champs to 45 days in jail for scaling the Las Vegas Sphere in an anti-abortion stunt.

events.title: Gilgo Beach killer Rex Heuermann's ex-wife says she now lives in basement where he murdered seven victims
events.description: Gilgo Beach killer Rex Heuermann's ex-wife reveals in a docuseries that she moved into the basement where he admitted to killing seven of his victims.

events.title: WATCH: Masked thieves wielding hammers hit jewelry store in what expert calls 'mob-style' heist
events.description: Alleged thieves caught on video fleeing a Texas jewelry store after a mob-style heist involving hammers, pepper spray and stolen display cases.

events.title: Republicans cut Democrats out entirely with marathon overnight vote on border cash and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Man who pleaded guilty to raping 12-year-old relative is illegal immigrant from Honduras, DHS says
events.description: Jose Lopez-Montoya, an illegal immigrant from Honduras, pleaded guilty to raping and impregnating a 12-year-old relative and faces up to 99 years in prison.

events.title: Florida prisoner laughs as judge sentences him to life for killing cellmate with pen: 'You are amusing'
events.description: A Florida inmate smiled and laughed in court as a judge sentenced him to life in prison for killing his cellmate by stabbing him in the head with a pen.

events.title: Party prep tale falls apart as husband charged in wife’s decades-old cold case murder
events.description: Authorities say Dwight Rust Jr. is responsible for his wife Michelle Rust's 2002 cold case murder. He faces first-degree murder charges in Maryland.

events.title: Husband of American woman who vanished in the Bahamas hires US lawyer after returning from island
events.description: Brian Hooker has retained a Michigan-based attorney after returning to the U.S. as the search for his wife Lynette Hooker continues in the Bahamas.

events.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
events.description: The ICC confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for allegedly overseeing deadly drug crackdowns.

events.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
events.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.
